item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as no source found indicates the image has been released under Creative Commons. Thus, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was wondering wouldn't this scp be euclid? I mean i understand why it's safe but it said that one of the class D's got a cut. Sorry if I sound dumb I'm sorta new to this scp stuff. Anyway I like this scp.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Damn. Mr. Gurgles is a crankypants.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This part:

Leucotomy performed at [DATA EXPUNGED]

Disgusted and disturbed me more than anything in the article itself.

Also:

Operation Durden is a system of scanning computers…

the keywords were ‘imaginary friend’

I see what you did there, sir.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
but since I never read responses to my own comments

…wait, so all the questions you've asked in your comments are essentially rhetorical?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'd ask for a link to any tales that might tell the backstories hinted at here in the discussion, but since I never read responses to my own comments that would be utterly pointless. I'll go digging later.

As for the scip itself, I feel like it leans too heavily on the interviews. The description ought to be, well, a description of the object's anomalous behaviors - this is a scientific document IU, not an entertainment piece. Upvoted all the same, but that bothered me a bit. Just saying.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, that was. A thing. Hm. I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As with all of the articles that I have written, I have an awful lot of backstory and explanation that I haven't put in, as it's unrealistic that the researchers would know it, and it would not be pertinent to containment procedures and therefore not required. In my headcanon there are a lot of D-class that have ended up there through no fault of their own, and the Foundation is directly involved in their acquisition.

As for the common factor, you're spot on - Gurgles requires suggestibility as he has some very specific requirements to pull free of the dimensional doorway that he's currently stuck in. (Yes, Gurgles is WINNIE THE POOH!!) that someone less persuadable would immediately refuse.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The SCP entity itself is interesting in its own right, but it's the first interview that poses the most interesting questions. Specifically, the implication that the woman was made a condemned prisoner and from there a became D-Class for a crime she didn't commit. (Even with your explanation I'm unsure as to what happened, though - who held her down and forced her to watch? Her parents? An unknown SCP?) For that matter, given how many SCP's there are out there which cause inexplicable deaths, how many other innocent people have been headed for the clink because there was no other apparent explaination?

On a side note, BTW, what's the common factor for who the entity/entities choose to talk to? The boy wasn't mentally disabled and low age doesn't equal low IQ, and the D-classes weren't children. If it's a factor of how easy they're likely to be to manipulate, to convince to do something they shouldn't, would it work with someone with someone with autism? Autistics are frequently very intelligent but lack a hell of a lot of the filters that let people weed out certain bad ideas.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really struggles with the description, I knew I wanted a lot to come out in the interviews, so I kept cutting down the references to the anomaly. I think 'vocalisation' was the driest I could get it. I would welcome suggestions on how to improve it without losing the slow build.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very deliberate. And no, nobody told her the name. I think I'm writing a Tale to go with this one. :-)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very nicely done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is it intentional that the D-class referred to the voice as Mr. Gurgles as well? As in, did staff tell her it was a voice named Mr. Gurgles, or did it tell her that, or did she somehow come up with the same name?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is another article I upvoted for the interviews, but in this case, the description really does this no favors. I was ready to downvote and then decided to click the collapsibles, just to be fair. All the interesting stuff was in there. I'm not really sure what I could suggest to change things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think I agree, I did write that a bit melodramatically. I've taken out the pause.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm from (pauses) England, yes.

Something about the pause rubs me the wrong way. It reminds me too much of Dracula saying "I do not drink (pauses) wine".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh no the SCP was good. I like the disembodied voice that (apparently accidentally) gouges the fuck out of people that go poking around in its domain, and apparently wants something unthinkable done/given so it can go "home".

It's just the whole, "held down and made to watch [INSERT HERE] drown your twin sister and the whole world will think you did it" made my brain spin into a corkscrew.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eeeenh, something about this rubs me the wrong way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, I wouldn't put that in, I've learned my lesson about referencing other SCPs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Don't do it, Gurgles is better than a Montauk reference.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like that a lot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And of course I now want to know exactly what that is. I can see it now:

As of [DATE EXPUNGED], SCP-1140 has been reclassified as Keter. All access to it by supposedly "receptive" individuals is hereby forbidden by O5 Command.

- How in the hell does it know about 110-Montauk….

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As I said above, I think I'm going to have to do some short stories about Gurgles, and probably SCP-1113 as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
backstory sent more of a chill up my spine than the actual SCP

Something went wrong there then! :) What did you not like about the SCP?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One of the things I struggle a lot with is that I want to put far more backstory into the article than would be prudent - after all, it's supposed to be an official report with basic details. At some point I'm going to start writing some creepypastas about my SCPs before they were retrieved to get it all out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know how to weave a helluva backstory.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And now I suspect that her telling someone why she didn't help is why she's a Class D. Gave me shivers, and I want to +1 all over again. Kudos!

Who knows, maybe the bath thing is an SCP in itself

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
……….. Holy crap this tease at a backstory sent more of a chill up my spine than the actual SCP

Edit: I have found that I needed to pry my stuck i key loose.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: He's very hesistant indeed. He almost got through completely once, but even a toddler realises that doing that was wrong.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was about to kick off then - I looked at the edit history and thought for a moment that Voct had deleted and rewritten the entire description. Then I realised it's just the comparison tool being weird, and he had in fact made a couple of small but very wise changes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmmm, good point, so why hasn't he made any demands yet? Does he know he's being monitored and that's why he remains hesitant?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well you'll notice that he does speak to the researcher once. His demands are unlikely to be met by someone of sound mind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: He chooses to talk to children? That's a little less interesting personally but it could be interesting all the same.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh, I was being silly. Ignore me.

On the subject of them being too stupid to help him, notice that Gurgles chooses to only talk to people with low mental capabilities.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: OK, just for you (oh, and everyone else who reads this) she was sectioned at 12 years old after her twin sister drowned while they were sharing a bath. The one question her doctors, her parents, everyone asked, was why didn't she help her? Why did she watch her drown? They weren't there though. They weren't the ones that had been held down and made to watch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Now, I normally have a backstory behind my SCPs, with more details than are actually in the text, but I have to admit that I have no idea why she called him Mr Gurgles as well. I think it's probably something akin to his name, and that receptive enough brains kind of absorb it, but don't get it quite right.

Edit: I missed a trick - if it had been Yog-Soggoth, his imaginary friend name could have been Yoghurt Socks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, by the way: Does everyone spontaneously call him/it "Mr. Gurgles"? Because both the first kid and the mentally retarded girl called him/it that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: More along the lines of "powerful ancient that was trapped in a horrible situation" than "powerful ancient stuck in house-stuff". But yeah, he's trapped in a bad situation and his only chance of escape is too stupid to help him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm, Janus, that hadn't come to mind. That's a good comparison, apart from the drawers bit. Janus wouldn't be found dead in furniture.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The original one was a mistake, I think in an earlier draft it was 3 and above, before I realised that that wasn't really very 'Top Seekrit' at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As I said before, I love Mr. Gurgles, he reminds me of Janus in a good way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Putting it in a report like this would be stupid though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: High praise from you. Thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not bad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nicely done, as I said previously. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have changed that, but one part of me wants to go 'Only 5 levels, eh? That's what they WANT you to think!' and then cackle maniacally.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. But "information for Level 5 and above" is wrong: there ARE only 5 levels of clearance, with the O5 council (Overseer Level 5) being the only ones w/ top clearance to have access to everything.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm still wondering who this 'her' the D-class didn't help is. But I like it! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For anyone who is curious, in my brain the room numbering R.4.13 designates Wing R of the Site, Floor 4, Storage area 13, and wing R is for Safe inanimates.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. Keep up the good work
